front: State the Rank-Nullity Theorem for a linear transformation T: V → W
back: For a linear transformation T: V → W, dim(null(T)) + dim(range(T)) = dim(V). The sum of the dimension of the kernel and the dimension of the image equals the dimension of the domain.
Rank of a matrix is the dimension of its column space (or row space). It represents the maximum number of linearly independent columns (or rows) in the matrix.
Nullity of a matrix is the dimension of its null space (kernel). It is the number of free variables in the solution to Ax = 0.

front: What is the relationship between rank and nullity for an m×n matrix?
back: For an m×n matrix A, rank(A) + nullity(A) = n, where n is the number of columns (dimension of the domain).

front: Define the four fundamental subspaces of a matrix A (m×n)
back: 1. Column space C(A) ⊆ ℝ^m, 2. Null space N(A) ⊆ ℝ^n, 3. Row space C(A^T) ⊆ ℝ^n, 4. Left null space N(A^T) ⊆ ℝ^m

front: What is the dimension of the column space equal to?
back: The dimension of the column space equals the rank of the matrix, which is also equal to the dimension of the row space.

front: When is a linear transformation T: V → W one-to-one?
back: T is one-to-one (injective) if and only if null(T) = {0}, i.e., nullity(T) = 0. Equivalently, rank(T) = dim(V).

front: When is a linear transformation T: V → W onto?
back: T is onto (surjective) if and only if range(T) = W, i.e., rank(T) = dim(W).

front: What is the rank of a matrix if it has linearly independent columns?
back: If an m×n matrix has n linearly independent columns, its rank = n (full column rank), and nullity = 0. Such a matrix is one-to-one.

front: What is the nullity of a matrix if it has linearly independent rows?
back: If an m×n matrix has m linearly independent rows (full row rank), its rank = m, and nullity = n - m.

front: Define null space (kernel) of a linear transformation
back: The null space (or kernel) of T: V → W is the set of all vectors in V that map to zero in W: null(T) = {v ∈ V | T(v) = 0}.

front: Define range (image) of a linear transformation
back: The range (or image) of T: V → W is the set of all vectors in W that are images of some vector in V: range(T) = {T(v) | v ∈ V}.

front: What is a full rank matrix?
back: A matrix has full rank if its rank equals the minimum of its number of rows and columns. For m×n matrix, full rank means rank = min(m,n).

front: How does rank relate to solving Ax = b?
back: Ax = b has solutions if and only if b is in the column space of A. If rank(A) = rank([A|b]), the system is consistent.

front: What is the dimension of the row space equal to?
back: The dimension of the row space equals the rank of the matrix, which is the same as the dimension of the column space.
